com.tonic.jaloc.impl.sets

Class PFloatSet

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.sets.PFloatSet
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PFloatSet
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.AutoCloseable
    A float set over bit patterns in a PIntSet; NaNs collapse to one element, -0.0 and 0.0 stay distinct.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PFloatSet()
      Creates an empty set on the system allocator.
      PFloatSet(long expectedElements)
      Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the system allocator.
      PFloatSet(NativeAllocator allocator, long expectedElements)
      Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the given allocator.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean add(float value)
      Adds value.
      void clear()
      Empties the set.
      void close()
      Releases the backing native memory.
      boolean contains(float value)
      Tests whether value is present.
      void forEach(FloatConsumer consumer)
      Emits each present value in no particular order.
      boolean isEmpty() 
      boolean isOpen() 
      boolean remove(float value)
      Removes value.
      long size() 
      void trim()
      Shrinks the table to the smallest size fitting the current elements.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PFloatSet

        public PFloatSet()
        Creates an empty set on the system allocator.

      • PFloatSet

        public PFloatSet(long expectedElements)
        Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        expectedElements - presizes the table
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if expectedElements is negative

      • PFloatSet

        public PFloatSet(NativeAllocator allocator,
                 long expectedElements)
        Creates an empty set presized for expectedElements on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        expectedElements - presizes the table
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if expectedElements is negative

    • Method Detail

      • add

        public boolean add(float value)
        Adds value.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to add
        Returns:
        true if the set changed
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • remove

        public boolean remove(float value)
        Removes value.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to remove
        Returns:
        true if the set changed
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • contains

        public boolean contains(float value)
        Tests whether value is present.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to test
        Returns:
        true if present
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • size

        public long size()
        Returns:
        the element count

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Returns:
        true if no elements are present

      • isOpen

        public boolean isOpen()
        Returns:
        true until closed

      • forEach

        public void forEach(FloatConsumer consumer)
        Emits each present value in no particular order.
        Parameters:
        consumer - the receiver
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if consumer is null
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • clear

        public void clear()
        Empties the set.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • trim

        public void trim()
        Shrinks the table to the smallest size fitting the current elements.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • close

        public void close()
        Releases the backing native memory. Safe to call more than once.
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
